_meta.taxonomy: 8-category (Correctness Bugs, Security, Race Conditions, Failure Modes, Operability, Scalability, Design Debt, Underspecification)
_meta.note: Faithful JSON serialization of the human-readable synthesis (gauntlet-concerns-2026-06-16.md) produced by the one-Opus-pass step. Pipeline verdicts treated as advisory; re-judged from scratch. This v8 delta-attack drove the v9->v12 redesign (notably CB-1 status/tombstone/supersedes, CB-2 run_evidence union, CB-3 live_or_induced, GateResult outcome-only).

concerns.title: Tombstone/status field missing from the canonical schema
concerns.detail: §7 says tombstoned/renamed TMRs no longer satisfy F'; §4.2 canonical field list has no status/tombstoned_at/supersedes; stale spine:true keeps covering a US after delete/supersede.

concerns.title: run_evidence has 2-3 incompatible shapes; no discriminated union
concerns.detail: §3.1, §8.1 tier-shaped receipts, and TC-8.0/keystone third shape cannot all validate under extra:forbid; needs explicit discriminated union.

concerns.title: live_or_induced regressions the convergence just fixed (continues R6-C7)
concerns.detail: TC-8.0 exemplar still scalar; JSON null overloaded between justified-MOCK and a no-fault REAL-DATA happy path; needs REAL-DATA-vs-MOCK disambiguation.

concerns.title: MOCK falsification rule only covers literal MOCK
concerns.detail: MOCK-EXTERNAL/SYNTHETIC/FRONTEND/STATIC dodge the §4.3 rule; rule must cover every non-REAL strategy.

concerns.title: env-trust matrix (criticality x env -> trusted?) referenced but absent
concerns.detail: §8.1 close behavior depends on a table that does not exist; implementers will each invent one.

concerns.title: Duplicate JSON keys bypass validation
concerns.detail: Standard JSON loaders discard earlier duplicate keys before schema validation; TmrParser needs a dup-preserving parse.

concerns.title: tmr_uid generation/uniqueness/collision undefined
concerns.detail: Who allocates, uniqueness scope, collision handling, copy-paste duplication all unspecified; anchored in an HTML comment in prose.

concerns.title: tests-pseudo.md still self-declares Canonical source of truth
concerns.detail: §4.2 demotes it to generated-view-only (registry is SoR) but header and TC-8.0 still claim canonical authority -> dual-SoR residue.

concerns.title: Owner-repo command execution is an unsandboxed RCE/exfil surface
concerns.detail: §8.1 skill-runner executes owner-repo-declared command with no sandbox/allowlist/timeout/network-secret isolation.

concerns.title: Prompt injection into the LLM compiler and guardrail subagents
concerns.detail: Untrusted prose -> compiler emits spine:true / drops liveness; echo-diff+confirm catches shape not semantic deceit.

concerns.title: Fault-injection techniques can DoS the host / lack safety envelope
concerns.detail: external-kill/tc-netem/chmod 000/cgroups/state-injection without PID/namespace/resource scoping, preflight, or cleanup.

concerns.title: Subagent snapshot hashing is self-attestation, not proof (downgrade over-claim)
concerns.detail: §5.1 LLM-reported read-hashes can be omitted/hallucinated; pinned snapshot is defense-in-depth, not a mechanical guarantee. Instrument reads at tool layer or relabel best-effort.

concerns.title: Local trust-anchor hardening gaps (TOCTOU / path-traversal / tamperable checker)
concerns.detail: (a) TOCTOU between F' pass and lane advance (bytes not pinned through transition); (b) path traversal/symlink in checker inputs (no canonical-root check); (c) checker executable tamperable (Fizzy runs uv run gauntlet-check from a live symlink).

concerns.title: >=50-char override floor is a speed-bump, not proof
concerns.detail: lorem/aaaa passes; spec already frames as raising effort. Do not over-claim.

concerns.title: Snapshot manifest race on a shared branch
concerns.detail: main-loop no-edit doesn't bind other agents on the branch; files change mid-round -> mixed-snapshot findings or hash-thrash/deadlock. Needs git-tree/content snapshot, not honor-system hash report.

concerns.title: Only the journal got concurrency hardening; the SoR didn't
concerns.detail: §7 filelocks registry.journal.jsonl but tmr-registry.json (actual SoR), decisions.log, pending-dispositions.json get no locking.

concerns.title: Journal append not transactional with the registry update
concerns.detail: Atomic journal append but no atomic 'update registry + append journal' -> orphaned journal entry or registry change.

concerns.title: Non-overridable setup_error/schema_error is a DoS / strands hotfixes
concerns.detail: One malformed TMR/over-budget collector/Fizzy outage bricks the pipeline with no escape. Need bounded degradation or loud break-glass.

concerns.title: Fizzy outage = version-fence outage / offline DoS
concerns.detail: §8.2 fail-closes-as-post-fence when card timestamp unfetchable; no cache/retry/degraded mode -> can't work offline.

concerns.title: Orchestrator root-death has no detector
concerns.detail: expected_returns accounting assumes orchestrator survives; if it dies first nothing flags the missing round. Needs parent/wrapper contract.

concerns.title: Activation is a non-atomic two-repo rollout
concerns.detail: Skill + Fizzy constants + checker entry + integration test must land together; no deploy ordering/rollback/mixed-version (split-brain). R4-1 acknowledges the hole, not the choreography.

concerns.title: LLM compile can emit schema-valid but semantically-wrong registry records
concerns.detail: Hallucinated accessors/wrong user_story pass schema; human-confirm is the only backstop. Require compile to cite-resolve accessor symbols.

concerns.title: No observability for a fail-closed gate
concerns.detail: No metrics/SLO/runbook for override rate, fan-out, retries, lock waits, checker latency, block-reason distribution.

concerns.title: gauntlet-check is not an exposed entry point
concerns.detail: §6 specs `uv run gauntlet-check`; pyproject exposes only adversarial-spec; Fizzy-calls-local-skill-CLI is an unstable boundary.

concerns.title: Spec declares converged/locked while its own F' data path is red
concerns.detail: §13 honestly notes 14 spine tests aren't compiled into tmr-registry.json so F' wouldn't pass this draft; surface as a visible status caveat (R4-1 activation, §12.11).

concerns.title: Subagent fan-out / retry thundering-herd, no fleet retry budget
concerns.detail: 5 subagents x retries x concurrent sessions -> API bursts / 429 cascades.

concerns.title: Journal index contract undecided; registry is a hot key
concerns.detail: §7 rotated-with-retention OR indexed-immutable undecided; idempotency-by-scan O(N); registry read by many. Likely fine at current scale; flag for scale.

concerns.title: TestInputCollector over-budget hard-fails large repos (folds with FM-1)
concerns.detail: Needs sharding/paging/partial-with-loss, not absolute setup_error.

concerns.title: §12.1 is still open (undercuts LOCKED)
concerns.detail: Per-guardrail finding schema + parallel-subagent orchestration unresolved, yet K/conflict/journaling/aggregation depend on it.

concerns.title: decisions.log format conflicts with the live project contract
concerns.detail: §7/DD-9 mandates structured JSONL with decision_id; actual convention (and debate.py, and this session) appends plain text. No migration/back-compat.

concerns.title: also_covers non-covering forces redundant spine designations
concerns.detail: Cross-US journeys declare scalar spine:true per US; deliberately chosen for counting determinism; revisit if authoring pain shows.

concerns.title: Not a slice / multi-repo platform as one card (sequencing option)
concerns.detail: MINI ship-a-smaller-slice-first (schema + F' checker + stricter MOCK + Phase-8 live evidence; defer journal/subagents/classifier) is a legit phasing suggestion for execution.

concerns.title: Over-engineering / simpler-alternative suggestions (two worth adopting)
concerns.detail: Reuse decisions.log for pending dispositions (vs separate pending-dispositions.json); sequential guardrails with structured output (vs parallel subagents, ties to §12.1/DD-1).

concerns.title: Historical clutter (editorial, for finalize)
concerns.detail: Changelogs/superseded notes/resolved-OQs embedded throughout; finalize pass should emit the clean final contract.

concerns.title: Negative-oracle contract is vague
concerns.detail: §8.1 requires a negative oracle but not what counts; must fail for the same boundary the positive test exercises (not a trivial bad-input).

concerns.title: F' checker contract vs the MOCK/liveness validation it implies
concerns.detail: §6 checker = exactly one spine:true (structural), but prose implies REAL-DATA-or-justified; pin what the deterministic checker validates vs what is left to LLM guardrails.

concerns.title: Authoritative phase source undefined
concerns.detail: For the §4.4/§6 maturity-threshold logic.

concerns.title: ID hygiene: Unicode/case/zero-width in identifiers
concerns.detail: test_id/user_story/tmr_uid/decision_id hardening; lower priority than CB-7 uid allocation.

dismissed_representative.claim: F' accepts nl -> not a real liveness gate
dismissed_representative.reason: by design; nl passes debate->gauntlet, live proof deferred to Phase 8. Known, locked.

dismissed_representative.claim: Provenance journal is over-engineered DB (flat dismissal)
pipeline_advance not wired / checker doesn't exist yet
dismissed_representative.reason: that is the activation rule (R4-1), folded into FM-4 deploy-choreography.

cross_model_signal: Codex and gemini-flash independently converged on: SEC-1 owner-repo RCE, SEC-4 subagent-hash self-attestation, FM-1 fail-closed DoS, RC-1 snapshot race, CB-2 run_evidence schema fork, CB-4 MOCK-strategy escape, CB-5 env-trust matrix absence, DD-1 §12.1-still-open lock contradiction.
